dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: Sabre Corp — global travel-technology / GDS air-distribution + IT software.
dossiers.thesis: The equity is a thin sliver on a ~$4.6B enterprise where a real operational turn is now flowing against a shrinking-but-heavy debt load: six consecutive improving EPS prints, EBITDA and bookings inflecting while the stock is still priced like distressed debt-heavy equity ~40% below its prior high. Consensus underweights how EV re-rating + paydown compounds through ~5.5x leverage into the equity over 6-24 months.
dossiers.inflection_evidence: Q1 2026 (8-K acc 0001628280-26-031703): revenue $760M +8% YoY, Normalized Adj EBITDA $169M +21% (margin +235bps to 22.2%), air bookings +6% to 87M (2-yr high), EPS +$0.06 vs -$0.03 est (6th improving print); gross debt $5.2B(24)->$4.44B(Mar26).
dossiers.catalyst: FCF crossing positive in 2027 + continued EBITDA beats driving an EV/EBITDA re-rate through ~5.5x leverage; next print Q2 2026
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: Normalized Adj EBITDA grows YoY for the next 3 prints and gross debt falls further through FY2026.
dossiers.kill_condition: Adj EBITDA growth stalls/reverses on any print, or a debt maturity/refi shock
dossiers.adversarial: The interest burden IS the thesis: ~$450M+/yr interest (2025 refis at 11.125%) vs ~$585M EBITDA leaves razor-thin equity FCF; any EBITDA stall or GDS secular disintermediation (airline NDC/direct-connect bypass) impairs the levered equity fast and asymmetrically. Negative book equity today; can go to zero.
dossiers.blowup_note: $665M cash, >90% debt pushed to 2029+, no maturity wall; FY26 FCF ~breakeven ex-restructuring. Survives the hold.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: ACV Auctions — digital wholesale used-vehicle auction marketplace + data/inspection services.
dossiers.thesis: A washed-out net-cash marketplace where the real inflection is MARGIN, not volume: operating leverage + services monetization more than doubled adj EBITDA while the stock de-rated twice on volume guide-downs to ~1.35x EV/sales. Consensus prices a soft used-vehicle cycle as permanent; a volume reacceleration + continued Manheim/ADESA share gains re-rates EV/sales over 6-24mo. It sits LOW in its 52wk range (~26% up, -56% from high) while every arrived peer sits high in theirs.
dossiers.inflection_evidence: Adj EBITDA $59M FY2025 (>2x YoY on $760M rev +19%), guided $73-77M FY2026; Q1-26 (8-K acc 0001637873-26-000019) adj EBITDA $17M above guide, non-GAAP net income turned POSITIVE (+$7M), GAAP loss narrowing to -$11M — on units +3% / GMV +5% (operating leverage, not a volume S-curve).
dossiers.catalyst: A quarter where unit/GMV growth reaccelerates alongside another EBITDA beat, re-rating EV/S from ~1.35x toward ~2.5x; next print early Aug 2026
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: Adj EBITDA grows YoY each of the next 3 prints AND Marketplace units re-accelerate above +5% YoY within the hold.
dossiers.kill_condition: Two consecutive prints of decelerating units AND a cut to EBITDA guide
dossiers.kill_value: units decel + EBITDA guide cut x2
dossiers.adversarial: Volume growth has STALLED (units +3%, GMV +5%) on a macro used-vehicle cycle outside the company control; the market already de-rated twice on light guides (Nov-25 -40%, Feb-26 -25%). Another light guide retests the $4.50-5 lows — this is a margin-in-a-soft-cycle story, not the high-growth adoption S-curve the multiple needs.
dossiers.blowup_note: Net cash ~$141M ($341M cash vs $200M debt), positive adj EBITDA + non-GAAP NI — self-funding.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: CBIZ — accounting/tax/benefits/insurance advisory to middle-market businesses (Marcum-merged).
dossiers.thesis: A durable recurring-revenue professional-services roll-up that over-corrected ~55% on Marcum-integration indigestion. Now inflecting on three tracks at once — organic growth reaccelerating off a flat Q4, leverage falling 3.9x->3.4x toward <2.5x, and per-share compounding via heavy buybacks — at ~8.6x forward EPS and 1.0x book. Consensus prices the integration dip as permanent; the next 2-3 prints prove the merger integrated.
dossiers.inflection_evidence: Q1 2026: Financial-Services organic ~+4% ex a ~200bps transitory client-exit headwind (mgmt says diminishes H2), sequentially up from flat Q4; net leverage 3.4x from 3.9x YE2025; adj EPS guide RAISED to $4.00-4.10; ~3.9M shares (~$200M) repurchased in 4 months. (Q1 2026 10-Q, CIK 0000944148.)
dossiers.catalyst: Organic growth reaccelerating to the stated mid-single-digit exit rate as the 200bps headwind rolls off + leverage crossing <2.5x; visible Q2/Q3 2026
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: Organic revenue growth reaccelerates above 3% and net leverage falls below 3.0x within the next 3 prints.
dossiers.kill_condition: Organic growth stuck near 1% for two more prints while leverage fails to fall
dossiers.adversarial: The "transitory" 200bps drag proves structural — middle-market advisory demand softening and/or AI-driven fee compression in core tax/accounting keeps organic near 1%, at which point 3.4x leverage limits flexibility and the EPS growth is revealed as buyback engineering on a flat operating base.
dossiers.blowup_note: self-funding / profitable / positive FCF per the read; no going-concern, fraud, or delisting.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: Freshpet — fresh/refrigerated pet food, a structural-growth consumer brand taking share of the pet-food category.
dossiers.thesis: The market de-rated FRPT ~35% on a Q4-25 growth scare (8.6%) and capex fears, but the very next print flipped the thesis: Q1-26 volume reaccelerated to +14.6% while the company crossed into GAAP profit and positive FCF. A 15x-P/E fresh-pet compounder with a capex-peaking, self-funding profile is being priced as a broken growth story rather than one that just inflected on both volume and cash.
dossiers.inflection_evidence: Q1 2026 (8-K acc 0001611647-26-000008): net sales $297.6M +13.1% on volume +14.6% (price/mix -1.5%, real unit demand); net income $48.5M vs -$12.7M loss YoY; adj gross margin 46.9% vs 45.7%; FCF +$12.7M; FY26 sales-growth guide RAISED to 8-11%. Follows FY25 first full-year positive FCF.
dossiers.catalyst: Q2 2026 print confirming the volume reacceleration was not a one-off + FCF ramp as ~$150M capex peaks and normalizes -> re-rate off a trough 15x P/E
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: Volume growth stays double-digit and FCF stays positive across the next 2 prints; FY26 lands in/above the 8-11% guide.
dossiers.kill_condition: Volume growth rolls to high-single-digits AND FCF turns negative on capex
dossiers.adversarial: Growth may be structurally decelerating not merely dipping — Q4-25 was 8.6% and FY26 guide of 8-11% implies Q1 13% fades; if volume rolls toward high-single-digits as Mars and Nestle Purina push into fresh/refrigerated, the de-rate is justified and 15x on decelerating growth is not obviously cheap.
dossiers.blowup_note: self-funding / profitable / positive FCF per the read; no going-concern, fraud, or delisting.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: National Vision — value optical retail (America’s Best, Eyeglass World).
dossiers.thesis: A genuine multi-quarter margin turnaround on positive comps, still trading ~35% off its high on trough GAAP earnings with a fortress 0.8x-levered self-funding balance sheet. The market prices the post-Walmart-exit revenue hole and discretionary-optical fears as permanent; the operating-margin recovery toward mid-cycle is a durable value-retail re-rate that consensus underweights over 18-24 months.
dossiers.inflection_evidence: Adjusted operating margin 3.6% (FY24) -> 5.2% (FY25) -> 10.2% (Q1 2026, +210bps YoY); comps +5.9% FY25 and +4.4% Q1 2026 (multi-quarter); adj op income $65.5M -> $102.5M; adj EPS $0.34 -> $0.45. (Q1 2026 8-K accession 0001628280-26-034360.)
dossiers.catalyst: Back-half 2026 comp reacceleration once the website-replatform drag clears + continued margin recovery toward mid-cycle 7-8%
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: Adjusted operating margin holds above 7% for FY2026 and comps stay positive through the back half.
dossiers.kill_condition: Comps turn negative for two prints or adj op margin falls back below 6%
dossiers.adversarial: The margin gains are cost-discipline/mix-driven and may plateau; discretionary optical demand plus the residual post-Walmart revenue hole cap the topline, and the Q2 website-replatform comp stumble shows the recovery is not yet self-sustaining — a consumer softening could stall the re-rate on a 34x trough-P/E stock.
dossiers.blowup_note: self-funding / profitable / positive FCF per the read; no going-concern, fraud, or delisting.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: nCino — cloud banking software (loan origination, account opening, AI) for financial institutions.
dossiers.thesis: A washed-out vertical-SaaS leader (~-49%) whose real in-the-numbers inflection is a hard margin/FCF turn, not revenue: non-GAAP operating income +79% to a 28% margin and FCF +54% while the company buys back stock at ~14x FCF. The market prices decelerating subscription growth as terminal; early AI monetization is the call option on revenue re-acceleration that consensus is not paying for.
dossiers.inflection_evidence: Q1 FY27 (qtr ended 4/30/26, 8-K acc 000190273326000064): non-GAAP op income +79% YoY to $44.5M (28% margin, from ~17%); FCF +54% to $80.8M; FY27 FCF guide raised to $135-140M (~+65%); PS gross margin +1,100bps via AI tooling; repurchased 6.1M shares ($93.1M) — share count falling.
dossiers.catalyst: AI monetization (Banking Advisor usage +38x Oct->May) inflecting subscription growth back toward mid-teens; proof-points in the next 2-3 prints + continued buyback
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: Subscription revenue growth ticks up YoY within 3 prints while FCF stays on the raised $135-140M guide.
dossiers.kill_condition: Subscription growth decelerates further below 8% with no AI-upsell reaccel
dossiers.adversarial: Subscription growth is guided DOWN to ~10% (8% total, US-mortgage a drag) — the slow bleed of a maturing vertical SaaS. If AI upsell does not re-accelerate the top line, the margin expansion is a one-time cost-compression story with a ceiling and the multiple stays compressed; add founder-CEO transition risk.
citations: nCino Q1 FY27 8-K, SEC EDGAR CIK 1902733 accession 000190273326000064
dossiers.blowup_note: self-funding / profitable / positive FCF per the read; no going-concern, fraud, or delisting.
